questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\prod_{n=1}^{50}(x-n)^{n(51-n)} \\
& \ln (f(x))=\sum_{n=1}^{50} n(51-n) \ln (x-n)
\end{aligned}$
Differentiating both side w.r.t. $x$
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)}=\sum_{n=1}^{50} \frac{n(51-n)}{x-n} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{f^{\prime}(51)}{f(51)}=1275
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
STATEMENT 1: If $y=\sin \left(m \sin ^{-1} x\right)$ then $\left(1-x^2\right) y_{n+2}-(2 n+1) x y_{n+1}+\left(n^2-m^2\right) y_n=0$ [ $y_n$ is $n$th derivative of y w.r.t. x$]$
STATEMENT 2: $\frac{d^n}{d x^n}[u(x) v(x)]=u_n v_0+{ }^n C_1 u_{n-1} v_1+{ }^n C_2 u_{n-2} v_2+\ldots . .+{ }^n C_n u_0 v_n \quad\left[u_n, v_n\right.$ denote nth derivative of $u(x), v(x)$ w.r.t. $x$ ]
options: Both the statements are true and Statement 2 is correct explanation of Statement 1.
Both the Statements are true and Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false.
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $y=\sec \left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ at $x=1$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
By Chain rule
$\begin{aligned} & y=\sec \left(\tan ^{-1} x\right) \\ & \frac{d y}{d x}=\sec \left(\tan ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \tan \left(\tan ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \frac{1}{1+x^2} \\ & \left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{x=1}=\sqrt{2} \cdot 1 \cdot \frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $y=\sqrt{a^2-b^2 \cos ^2(\ln x)}$ then $x^2 y^2\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & y^2=a^2-b^2 \cos ^2(\ln x) \Rightarrow 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=2 b^2 \cos (\ln x) \sin (\ln x) \cdot \frac{1}{x} \\ & \therefore x^2 y^2\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=b^4 \cos ^2(\ln x) \sin ^2(\ln x)=b^4\left(\frac{a^2-y^2}{b^2}\right)\left(1-\frac{a^2-y^2}{b^2}\right)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Match the following List-I with List-II
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (P) } \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=2 x+g^{\prime}(1), f^{\prime \prime}(x)=2 \\ & g^{\prime}(x)=2 x+f^{\prime}(2), g^{\prime \prime}(x)=2 \\ & \text { at } x=1, f^{\prime}(1)=2+g^{\prime}(1) \text { and } g^{\prime}(1)=2+f^{\prime}(2) \\ & \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(1)=4+f^{\prime}(2)\end{aligned}$
$\text { (Q) } \therefore 2 \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{y})=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})+\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y})$

Replacing x by y and y by x , then
$2 f(y) f(x)=f(y-x)+f(y+x)$

From equation (1) and (2), we get $f(x-y)=f(y-x)$ Put $y=0$, then differentiate
(R)
$\begin{aligned}
& f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+2 x f^{\prime}(1)+f^{\prime \prime}(2) \\
& \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(1)^{\prime}=3+2 f^{\prime}(1)+f^{\prime \prime}(2) \\
& \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(1)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)=-3 \\
& \Rightarrow \text { and } f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 x+2 f^{\prime}(1) \\
& \therefore f^{\prime \prime}(2)=12+2 f^{\prime}(1) \\
& \therefore-2 f^{\prime}(1)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)=12
\end{aligned}$
(S) $n(a+b)^{n-1}=n a^{n-1}+n b^{n-1}$
